title: Splunk Monitoring Use Case
description: JSON-first authoring schema for a Splunk Monitoring Use Case (UC). Locked at Phase 1.1.

$schema.description: Optional IDE-tooling hint pointing to this schema. Ignored by the build; present so VS Code / JetBrains / Neovim LSP clients autocomplete fields.

id.description: UC identifier in the form category.subcategory.index, e.g. 22.35.1. The full UC-ID is 'UC-' + id.

subcategory.description: Explicit subcategory bucket key. Two formats are accepted: (1) the public '<category>.<subcategory>' form (e.g. '4.5'), used when a UC is intentionally cross-listed under a subcategory whose number does not match the id prefix — UC-4.4.32 'Function Cold-Start Latency Anomalies' lives under 4.5 'Serverless & FaaS' even though its id starts with 4.4; (2) the disambiguated '<category>.<subcategory>#<n>' form (e.g. '22.3#1'), used only when a category has multiple subcategories sharing the same numerical id and the UC belongs to the n-th occurrence (1-indexed; the first occurrence is just '22.3' with no suffix). The disambiguator preserves the legacy markdown 'extended clauses' grouping (e.g. '### 22.3 DORA' vs '### 22.3 — DORA (extended clauses)') without introducing new public ids. Omit when the id prefix already matches the first occurrence of the subcategory bucket.

title.description: Short human-readable title of the use case.

criticality.description: Risk / business impact if the monitored condition is not detected.

wave.description: Implementation maturity tier within a category (the 'crawl → walk → run' roadmap). 'crawl' = ship first: install the TA, turn on a base data feed, put one panel or alert in place. 'walk' = depends on one or two crawl UCs being live (typically same-category refinements: anomaly detection over a trend, cross-host correlation, SLA math on an existing signal). 'run' = depends on multiple crawls/walks, often cross-category (e.g. ITSI service trees that consume both host and app metrics). Curator-maintained; validated against 'prerequisiteUseCases' at build time so a 'crawl' UC cannot prerequisitely depend on a 'run' UC.

prerequisiteUseCases.description: UC ids (full 'UC-X.Y.Z' form, not bare 'X.Y.Z') that must be implemented before this UC produces value. Curator-maintained. Validated against the catalogue at build time: unknown ids fail the build, self-references fail the build, and cycles in the prerequisite graph fail the build (Kahn's topological sort). Wave monotonicity is enforced as a warning, not an error — a crawl UC depending on a run UC prints a build warning.

industry.description: Industry vertical this UC is specific to (e.g. 'Financial Services', 'Healthcare', 'Utilities'). Appears as '- **Industry:**' in the cat-22 markdown.

owner.description: Accountable role for this control. Drives evidence-pack roll-up.

controlFamily.description: Which of the 15 cross-regulation control families this UC belongs to. Empty only when the UC is regulation-specific and does not fit a cross-cutting family.

exclusions.description: Explicit scope exclusion. States what the UC does NOT cover. Required so reviewers and auditors know the limits.

evidence.description: Auditor-facing evidence artefact produced when this UC is active. Name a dashboard, saved-search, report, or index that an auditor can be shown.

evidenceSigning.description: Optional: how evidence produced by this UC is timestamped or signed. Enforced for production evidence packs in Phase 2 of the auditor-facing rollout.

compliance.description: Clause-level regulatory mappings. Optional at the schema level (operational and observability UCs typically have none); when present, must contain at least one entry. Cat-22 (regulatory compliance) UCs and any UC that claims to satisfy or detect a violation of a clause MUST populate this array. Use multiple entries to claim coverage across regulations.

regulation.description: Regulation identifier. Must match an entry in data/regulations.json. Examples: GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, NIST-800-53, ISO-27001, NIS2, DORA, SOX-ITGC, SOC-2, CMMC, NERC-CIP, NIST-CSF, FedRAMP, EU-AI-Act.

version.description: Version of the regulation (must be present in regulations.json versions[] for the named regulation). Examples: '2016/679' (GDPR), 'v4.0' (PCI DSS), 'Rev.5' (NIST 800-53), '2022' (ISO 27001).

clause.description: Specific clause / article / control ID as written in the authoritative source. Must match the clause_grammar regex for this regulation+version in regulations.json. Examples: 'Art.32(1)(b)' (GDPR), '§164.312(b)' (HIPAA), '10.2.1' (PCI DSS), 'AU-2' (NIST 800-53), 'A.8.15' (ISO 27001).

clauseUrl.description: Deep link to the specific clause in the authoritative source. When omitted, generated from regulations.json clause_url_template.

mode.description: satisfies: the UC demonstrates compliance with the clause. detects-violation-of: the UC fires when the clause is violated (e.g. 'no unencrypted PAN in transit' is detected by a UC firing on unencrypted traffic).

assurance.description: full: the UC is by itself sufficient evidence of the clause. partial: the UC is one of several controls needed to meet the clause. contributing: the UC is useful context but not a primary control. Weights: full=1.0, partial=0.5, contributing=0.25.

assurance_rationale.description: One-line explanation of why this assurance level was chosen. Explicit so reviewers can challenge the judgement.

provenance.description: Where did this mapping come from? Drives trust-level in the coverage report.

signedBy.description: Name (+ credential) of the SME who vetted this clause mapping. Used in data/provenance/sme-signoffs.json.

derivationSource.description: Populated by scripts/generate_phase3_3_derivatives.py when this entry was mechanically derived from a parent-regulation entry via the derivesFrom graph in data/regulations.json. Presence indicates provenance='derived-from-parent'; absence means the entry was hand-authored or came from a direct crosswalk. Generator keys entries on (regulation, version, clause); if a direct entry exists for the same key it wins and no derivation is written.

parentRegulation.description: Framework id of the parent regulation (e.g. 'gdpr').

parentClause.description: Clause id in the parent regulation that was the source of this derived mapping.

parentAssurance.description: Assurance level on the parent entry at the moment the derivation was generated. The inherited entry's own 'assurance' is degraded one step from this value (full → partial; partial → contributing; contributing never propagates).

inheritanceMode.description: Mirrors derivesFrom[derivative].inheritanceMode so downstream consumers can tell whether this derivative preserves the parent's clause numbers (identity) or renumbers them (mapped).

divergenceNote.description: Populated when derivesFrom[derivative].divergences lists the parent clause. Informational only — SME review should confirm whether the inherited mapping still holds given the divergence.

legalCaveat.description: Optional legal-counsel caveat attached to this clause entry. Populated when Phase 4.5b legal review returns outcome='conditional' or 'approved-with-revisions' and counsel requested that a specific caveat appear alongside the claim. Rendered in the compliance scorecard and in generated Splunk app help text. See docs/legal-review-guide.md §3.1.

smeCaveat.description: Optional subject-matter-expert caveat attached to this clause entry. Populated when Phase 5.2 SME review returns outcome='conditional' and the SME required a specific deployment-scope, data-source-version, or industry-scope caveat to appear alongside the claim. Typical content: field-extraction prerequisites, TA version pins, industry-specific applicability constraints. Rendered in the compliance scorecard and in generated Splunk app help text. See docs/sme-review-guide.md §4.1.

controlTest.description: Control-test definition. Without this, 'detects control X' is an unverifiable claim. Required for all tier-1 UCs; strongly recommended elsewhere.

positiveScenario.description: Narrative: what sequence of events SHOULD fire this UC. Testable against a sample-data fixture.

negativeScenario.description: Narrative: what looks similar but should NOT fire. Guards against false positives.

fixtureRef.description: Relative path to a sample-data fixture that exercises the positive scenario (e.g. 'sample-data/uc-22.35.1-fixture.json').

attackTechnique.description: MITRE ATT&CK technique ID (e.g. T1562.008). When present and the UC is tier-1, the Phase 4.5 QA gate runs the Atomic Red Team test for this technique and the UC must fire.

dataSources.description: Log / metric sources the SPL expects. Free-form narrative; may name indexes, sourcetypes, CIM nodes.

app.description: Primary Splunk app, add-on, or TA required. Free-form (e.g. 'Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)').

premiumApps.description: Splunk premium apps relied upon by this UC. Each entry is either a canonical enum string or an object that preserves the source spelling and any qualifier (e.g. 'optional, for asset/identity context').

name.description: Canonical short name used for machine lookup.

displayName.description: Verbatim source spelling (may be a long form like 'Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI)'). Used for markdown round-trip.

note.description: Qualifier extracted from a trailing parenthetical (e.g. 'optional, for asset/identity context').

spl.description: Primary Splunk search. Plain SPL (no triple-backtick fencing here).

cimSpl.description: Alternative SPL using tstats against accelerated CIM data models.

schema.description: Schema context for the UC. Examples: 'CIM', 'OCSF', 'OCSF: authentication'.

dataModelAcceleration.description: DMA note for implementers (e.g. 'Enable for Performance, Network_Traffic').

description.description: Plain-language description of what the UC detects or monitors.

value.description: Why this UC matters. Business-facing framing.

grandmaExplanation.description: Plain, jargon-free, 'explain it to my grandma' summary of what this use case does and why anyone should care. One to three sentences, no Splunk / SPL / CIM / MITRE / TA / add-on acronyms, no index / sourcetype / tstats, no regulation clause numbers. Uses 'we' voice (e.g. 'We watch...', 'We catch...', 'We help you know when...'). This is the primary UC text shown throughout the non-technical view. Generator-owned: populated by scripts/generate_grandma_explanations.py — do not hand-edit except to polish, and keep under the maxLength so the card and panel layouts do not overflow.

scriptExample.description: For scripted-input UCs: reference script body (typically fenced in markdown when rendered).

references.description: At least one authoritative link (regulator site, NIST publication, ISO page, vendor doc).

detectionType.description: Detection style or primary IOC/entity type. Two taxonomies coexist in the corpus: (1) SOC detection categories — 'TTP', 'Anomaly', 'Baseline', 'Hunting', 'Correlation' — and (2) IOC/entity types inherited from Splunk Enterprise Security risk-object conventions, e.g. 'system', 'user', 'process_name', 'ip_address', 'file_hash', 'url'. Free-form to preserve curator intent. The build pipeline does not perform taxonomy normalisation.

equipment.description: Structured equipment / platform tags (slugs matching the EQUIPMENT table in build.py — e.g. 'cisco', 'paloalto', 'azure', 'opcua'). Populated by scripts/generate_equipment_tags.py from substring-matching `app` + `dataSources` + `spl` + `implementation` fields. Source of truth when present; build.py's legacy substring match on the `app` field is used only as a fallback. See docs/equipment-table.md §4 for the canonical ID registry.

equipmentModels.description: Structured equipment-model compound tags in the form '<equipmentId>_<modelId>' (e.g. 'cisco_meraki', 'paloalto_globalprotect', 'hardware_bmc_idrac'). Drives the model sub-dropdown in the UI. Populated by scripts/generate_equipment_tags.py.

hardware.description: Free-form hardware / platform note. Mirrors the legacy '- **Hardware:**' markdown line and is preserved verbatim. Used for hardware-specific UCs (BMC, sensors, edge devices) where the deployment context matters but does not fit the structured equipment[] tags.

telcoUseCase.description: Free-form telco-vertical descriptor. Mirrors the legacy '- **Telco Use Case:**' markdown line. Surfaces in the cat-21 industry-vertical filter and is preserved verbatim so curator wording survives the round-trip through the build pipeline.

status.description: verified: production-ready, SME-signed-off. community: contributed, unreviewed. draft: work in progress.
